scenarios.title: Legacy environment remains supported with migration warning

files.text:
---
name: internal
description: internal
metadata:
  internal: true
---

divergences.expectation: Native distributions expose only open-skills and run with Node absent.
divergences.rationale: Avoid executable-name collisions and remove JavaScript as a runtime dependency.

divergences.expectation: Local shell and discovery guidance perform no automatic network or subprocess activity.
divergences.rationale: Routine local work must not phone home or retain a binary self-updater.

divergences.expectation: Insecure transports require an exact flag; redirects and diagnostics protect credentials.
divergences.rationale: Transport integrity and secret confinement are stronger than npm 0.1.2.

divergences.expectation: Remote Git installs resolve, record, and install one immutable commit.
divergences.rationale: Immutable provenance prevents branch movement between check and installation.

divergences.expectation: Repositories remain inert data; Git uses direct argv without hooks, submodules, or LFS smudge.
divergences.rationale: Untrusted repositories must not gain code-execution authority.

divergences.expectation: Discovery is bounded and ambiguous normalized names require exact selection.
divergences.rationale: Resource exhaustion and order-dependent skill selection are unsafe baseline behaviors.

divergences.expectation: --replace and --force authorize separate risks and --yes grants neither.
divergences.rationale: Provenance replacement and local-content loss require independent intent.

divergences.expectation: Mutations are journaled, recoverable, rolled back where practical, and advisory-locked with bounded waits.
divergences.rationale: Partial writes and concurrent corruption are data-loss risks.

divergences.expectation: Malformed and version-skewed state is preserved; supported unknown fields survive rewrites.
divergences.rationale: Silent state reset or downgrade can destroy provenance and extensions.

divergences.expectation: Remote agent instruction injection is trusted by sanitized source and exact commit, with offline audit and revocation.
divergences.rationale: Explicit exact-commit trust limits prompt-injection exposure.

divergences.expectation: Management JSON is deterministic, versioned, machine-only, prompt-free, and uses stable symbolic errors.
divergences.rationale: Automation must not parse decoration or infer risky decisions.

divergences.expectation: OPEN_SKILLS_* settings win exactly while legacy names remain warned through 1.x; risk flags stay independent.
divergences.rationale: Namespaced configuration and exact authorization provide deterministic migration behavior.

divergences.expectation: Native releases are immutable, checksummed, signed, provenanced, and never downloaded by npm.
divergences.rationale: The native supply chain must not preserve npm as a downloader or mutable trust root.
